items.title: Baby faces ongoing health concerns after birth delay in regional SA

children.content: A regional South Australian mother who faced a range of stressful diversions during her son's birth says she will always wonder whether his serious and ongoing health concerns were caused by delays in receiving care.

gallery.altText: Ashton Hurn sits on the grass playing with a baby being held by his mother while a 9yo girl sits nearby

thumbnail.altText: Ashton Hurn sits on the grass playing with a baby being held by his mother while a 9yo girl sits nearby

items.title: Former federal commissioner to lead review into scandal-plagued SA election

children.content: Former Australian electoral commissioner Tom Rogers is appointed to lead a "sweeping independent review" of the management and oversight of last month's SA state election and First Nations Voice election.

image.altText: A man with white hair, wearing a blue suit and glasses

items.title: Farmers and authorities on alert as locusts migrate to South Australia

children.content: Multiple locust swarms in South Australia's Riverland region have put farmers and authorities on alert, prompting the South Australian Plague Locust Commissioner to send surveying teams to the region.

image.altText: A person leaning out the window of a vehicle captures a paddock with thousands of locusts

items.title: Doctors fear new hospital design has 'increased risk of death' for women

children.content: Doctors say parts of the proposed design for Adelaide's $3.2 billion new Women's and Children's Hospital raise "significant concerns" including the location of the women's assessment service.

gallery.altText: A woman with shoulder-length blonde hair and glasses looks ahead.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with shoulder-length blonde hair and glasses looks ahead.

items.title: Adelaide motorists fined nearly $700,000 at new 40km/h school zones

children.content: The effectiveness of new speed limit signage has been questioned after the RAA revealed 1,100 people had been fined in just over one school term at two new 40km/h zones.

image.altText: Illuminated speed limit sign that says 40

items.title: Premier says algal bloom 'gone' but fishing restrictions need to expand

children.content: Fishing restrictions will be strengthened in South Australia due to the algal bloom's devastating impact on stocks, but the government says the bloom itself has "essentially cleared up".

image.altText: Professor Mike Steer speaking to the media in the foreground with Premier Peter Malinauskas in the background

items.title: Mother's impassioned plea after young daughter's cerebral palsy diagnosis

children.content: Ayla doesn't let disability slow her down, but her mother wants her to have the same opportunities as others.

image.altText: A girl in a blue and white dress, with pink glasses smiling while sitting in a park

items.title: Subdivisions taking place in Adelaide's 'visionary' Hills Face Zone

children.content: The Hills Face Zone was legislated to protect the natural character of Adelaide's landscape backdrop but, despite subdivisions not being permitted, there have been 11 over the past decade.

image.altText: Face of hillside stretching into distance with some houses among trees

items.title: Greyhound Racing SA employees fired after investigation into alleged wagering activity

children.content: Greyhound Racing SA says two of its employees from the integrity team have been stood down after an investigation into alleged wagering activity.

image.altText: Eight greyhounds race down a sand track at Cannington.

items.title: Wheelchair user calls for 'common sense' after using accessible zone without permit

children.content: Disability advocate and wheelchair user Shane Hryhorec is calling for Adelaide Airport to use a common sense approach after being threatened with a fine for using an accessible pick-up zone without a permit.

image.altText: A man in a wheelchair with his service dog on his lap

items.title: SA Police search waterways for knives linked to alleged murder of Caleb Leriano

children.content: SA Police will search waterways in Adelaide's south today to find three knives they believe are connected to the alleged murder of 24-year-old Caleb Leriano.

image.altText: A young man with glasse smiles at the camera in a bright room

items.title: This plant is the only known wild specimen in the world, found on just 300m of roadside

children.content: Only one known single spyridium plant is left in the wild, in crushed limestone on the side of a road. But scientists are hunting for more.

image.altText: The small fuzzy white flower of the Woods Well Spyridium

items.title: A simple message of love: Memorial held at Port Arthur 30 years on from the massacre

children.content: Thirty years on from the Port Arthur tragedy, survivor Jane Scholefield had a simple message for those still bearing the scars of that day — keep love at the centre of mourning.

items.title: 'You're lying': Rebel Wilson denies evidence is a 'grotesque pretence'

items.title: 'Almost bullying': Private hospitals blame closures on 'cowboy' insurers

children.content: A former Bupa executive accuses health insurers of using "almost bullying" tactics in their negotiations with private hospitals, while the Australian Private Hospitals Association says insurers have become "cowboys" in need of regulation.

items.title: Coalition points to stealth bombers as potential AUKUS stop-gap

items.title: Man who randomly killed British tourist not criminally responsible

children.content: A man who killed a British tourist in a random stabbing at an Albury motel in 2024 has been found not criminally responsible for the attack.

image.altText: Police car parked out the front of a motel.

items.title: Netball's powerful statement on Anzac Day

children.content: They both struggle for recognition in male-dominated fields, so Super Netball chose to host an all-female Anzac Day ceremony to commemorate and give visibility to female veterans.

items.title: The soy sauce fish is a perfect design — except on one important point

items.title: How to live on other mobs' Country, respectfully

children.content: To better understand what respect looks like in practice, I spoke with other First Nations people about how they approach working and living on other mobs' Countries.

items.title: Affidavit reveals more details as man charged with attempted Trump assassination

items.title: Perth man pleads not guilty to father's murder on grounds of insanity

items.title: Queensland waste company to fight allegations of nuisance odours

items.title: Protester who dumped shark heads at minister's office willing to go to jail

items.title: Will 500 homes in regional WA help solve the housing crisis?

children.content: The West Australian government has announced it plans to build 500 homes for public servants in regional areas over five years despite a "critical worker shortage".

image.altText: A small bobcat in front of a construction site of a modular house

items.title: Police honour Kalgoorlie detectives brutally killed 100 years ago

children.content: On this day in 1926, Detective Inspector John Walsh and Detective Sergeant Alexander Pitman were shot while trying to apprehend gold thieves near Kalgoorlie. Their charred and dismembered bodies were later found at the bottom of a mine shaft.

An historic black and white image of police investigating a murder case.
